title: Pink Molecules employee network forms in Basel
description: Gay and lesbian employees of the chemical and pharmaceutical industry in Basel launch Pink Molecules, a workplace network for LGB staff in the sector.

title: Pink City network of Zurich city-administration staff forms
description: Gay and lesbian employees of the Zurich city administration organize themselves under the name Pink City, creating an LGB staff network within the municipal workforce.

title: Woolf n' Wilde club starts at Swiss Re in Zurich
description: Gay and lesbian employees of the reinsurer Swiss Re in Zurich form Woolf n' Wilde, a staff club for LGB colleagues at the company.

title: Youth group Young LesBiSchwul starts in Zug
description: A youth group named Young LesBiSchwul is set up in Zug, giving lesbian, bisexual and gay young people in the canton a place to meet.

title: First Rosarote Adrian award presented in Bern
description: Bern sees the debut of the Rosarote Adrian, an award honoring gay and lesbian people and meeting places. The inaugural prize goes to Queersicht, the city's lesbian-gay film festival.

title: Club ZABI in Zurich closes
description: The Zurich club ZABI shuts its doors, ending its run as a venue for the city's gay scene.

title: BSM men's group founded in Basel
description: The association BSM (Begegnung Schwuler Männer) is established in Basel as a meeting group for gay men.

title: Zurich cantonal parliament passes same-sex partnership act
description: The cantonal parliament of Zurich adopts a progressive partnership act for same-sex couples, clearing the way for a cantonal popular vote on the law later that year.

title: HAZ launches discussion group for bisexual men
description: The Zurich organization HAZ (Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Zürich) starts a discussion group for bisexual men, adding a dedicated bi space to its program.

title: Zurich holds CSD under the motto We are family
description: Zurich's Christopher Street Day parade takes place with the motto "We are family", bringing the annual demonstration through the city once more.

title: Lesbian & Gay Pride held in Neuchâtel during Expo 02
description: Neuchâtel hosts a Lesbian & Gay Pride on the occasion of Expo 02, the Swiss national exhibition, bringing the march to the exhibition city.

title: Star march converges on the center of the canton of Zurich before partnership vote
description: Gay and lesbian demonstrators stage a first star march to the geographic midpoint of the canton of Zurich, campaigning for the cantonal partnership act put to voters on 22 September.

title: Zurich voters approve same-sex partnership law in world-first referendum
description: Voters in the canton of Zurich accept the law on equal rights for same-sex partnerships with 62.7 percent in favor. It is the first time anywhere that such a law is created by popular vote.

title: Exhibition on lesbian and gay history opens at Zurich city hall
description: The exhibition "Unverschämt", on the past and present of lesbians and gay men, opens at the Stadthaus in Zurich. It runs from 11 October 2002 to 18 January 2003.

title: Swiss Federal Council presents final bill for registered partnerships
description: The Federal Council submits its definitive draft of the registered-partnership act to parliament, a procedural step toward the national law for same-sex couples.

title: UBS employees start the pride@UBS network
description: Gay, lesbian and bisexual employees of the bank UBS set up an informal network under the name pride@UBS, later known as UBS Pride Switzerland.

title: Pilot issue of the magazine gay.ch appears
description: The magazine gay.ch publishes its issue zero, covering lifestyle, family, parties and people. From 2003 it appears four times a year, from 2005 six times.

title: Rainbowline Romandie counseling phone line starts
description: The Rainbowline Romandie goes into service in French-speaking Switzerland as a telephone counseling line for lesbians, gay men and mixed groups.

title: Youth groups found DIAGONAL as national umbrella organization
description: The youth groups Spot25 (Zurich), Rose (Basel-Landschaft), ComingInn (Bern) and Whynot (Lucerne) create DIAGONAL, a national umbrella body for lesbian, bi and gay youth groups that also offers online counseling for young people.

title: Zurich campaign association forms to back the partnership law
description: Gay and lesbian organizations in Zurich join forces in the association "Ja zum Partnerschaftsgesetz" to campaign for the partnership legislation.

title: Lesbian and gay groups in Graubünden unite as HAGR
description: The various lesbian and gay groups of the canton of Graubünden — among them COD Graubünden, Rosa Telefon, Aids-Hilfe Graubünden and Capricorn — join together as the Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Graubünden (HAGR).

title: Student group LAGO founded at the University of Fribourg
description: Students at the University of Fribourg establish LAGO, a lesbian and gay organization on campus.

title: Mythengay group forms in Schwyz
description: A group named Mythengay comes together in Schwyz, giving homosexual people in the canton a local point of contact.

title: Lucerne debuts the Pink Panorama film festival
description: Lucerne holds its first queer film festival under the name Pink Panorama. The event goes on to take place annually.

title: Freelife organization starts in Wil for eastern Switzerland
description: In Wil, canton of St. Gallen, the gay and lesbian organization Freelife is launched to promote tolerance toward homosexual people in eastern Switzerland.

title: Collegati collective forms in Ticino
description: The Collettivo Lesbico Gay Ticino, known as Collegati, comes into being in the Italian-speaking canton of Ticino.

title: European Court of Human Rights ranks anti-gay discrimination with racial discrimination
description: The European Court of Human Rights holds that discrimination against homosexual and bisexual people is as serious as discrimination based on race, origin, skin color or sex.

title: Traveling exhibition derBUS begins its tour in Dagmersellen
description: The traveling exhibition derBUS is shown for the first time in Dagmersellen, canton of Lucerne, presenting the lives of lesbians and gay men to mainly rural audiences. Its tour through 15 cantons lasts over five years, closing in Zurich on 18 May 2008.

title: RADIGAL forms as LGBTI section of the Swiss liberal party
description: RADIGAL is established as the LGBTI specialist group of the Swiss liberal party FDP and its youth wing, anchoring queer advocacy inside the party.

title: Canton of Zurich opens partnership registration to binational couples
description: From this day, binational same-sex couples in the canton of Zurich may register their partnership even while the foreign partner's residence status remains unresolved.

title: Gay and lesbian choir festival fills two Zurich concert halls
description: The SchwuLesbisches Chorspektakel, a festival of gay and lesbian choirs, takes place at the Volkshaus and the Tonhalle in Zurich over four days.

title: Zurich CSD marches under the motto landauf - stadtab
description: Zurich's Christopher Street Day takes place with the motto "landauf - stadtab", linking city and countryside in its message.

title: Zurich partnership law takes effect with first couple registered
description: The cantonal act on same-sex partnerships enters into force in Zurich. A first couple is officially registered at the Stadthaus, the earliest legal registration of a same-sex partnership in Switzerland.

title: Lesbian & Gay Pride held in Delémont
description: Delémont, capital of the canton of Jura, hosts a Lesbian & Gay Pride, bringing the march to the young canton.

title: First tri-national CSD 3Land held in Basel
description: Basel stages the first CSD "3Land" for the Regio Basiliensis, the border region spanning Switzerland, Germany and France.

title: HAZ sets up working group on schools
description: The Zurich organization HAZ forms a working group on schools, with subgroups for gay and lesbian teachers in the canton, education policy, and classroom visits.

title: Petition for a Vaud partnership law handed in at Lausanne
description: The organizations VoGay and Lilith submit a petition with 3,000 signatures in Lausanne, demanding a cantonal partnership law for same-sex couples.

title: Gay and lesbian groups meet for a hike on the Jakob Stutz trail
description: For a day of encounters between gay and lesbian groups, participants take a guided hike along the Jakob Stutz trail from Pfäffikon to Wila in the Tösstal, canton of Zurich.

title: National campaign body Ja zur Partnerschaft founded
description: Pink Cross, network, LOS and FELS establish the national association "Ja zur Partnerschaft" to campaign for the Swiss registered-partnership act.

title: First official church blessing of a same-sex couple in Zurich
description: On Coming Out Day, the first official church wedding ceremony for a same-sex couple takes place in the Reformed Predigerkirche in Zurich. The same day, the parish board of Winterthur-Seen refuses such a ceremony for the canton's second registered couple.

title: GayRadio starts broadcasting on Radio RaBe in Bern
description: A fortnightly program named GayRadio, today's QueerUp Radio, goes on air on the community station Radio RaBe in Bern. It can later also be heard on Radio LoRa in Zurich.

title: Groups hold first coordination meeting on youth and schools
description: Swiss groups working on the topic of youth and schools come together for their first coordination meeting, pooling efforts on education outreach.

title: Swiss National Council approves registered-partnership bill
description: The National Council adopts the federal act on registered partnerships for same-sex couples by 118 votes to 50, moving the bill forward in the parliamentary process that leads to the 2005 national referendum.

title: Queer gardeners organize as Querbeet
description: Gay, lesbian, bi and transgender gardeners form the group Querbeet, which belongs to the workplace section of Pink Cross.

title: Tödigay group forms in the canton of Glarus
description: Homosexual people from across the canton of Glarus come together in a group named Tödigay, after the canton's highest mountain.

title: Youth group Caméléon founded in Winterthur
description: A youth group named Caméléon is set up in Winterthur for young queer people in the region.

title: Les Homos group in Schaffhausen disbands
description: The group Les Homos in Schaffhausen dissolves, ending its activity in the canton.

title: Final conference held at Boldern
description: The last in the long-running series of conferences for homosexual people at the Boldern center above Lake Zurich takes place, closing a decades-old meeting tradition.

title: Central Switzerland conference launches its magazine UPDATE
description: UPDATE, the magazine and mouthpiece of the LesBiSchwule Konferenz of central Switzerland, appears for the first time, with five issues planned per year.

title: Group annersoberwallis founded in Brig
description: In Brig, canton of Valais, the group annersoberwallis is established for gay men and lesbians in the Upper Valais region.
